content: India has introduced the Index of Services Production (ISP), the country's first high-frequency indicator dedicated to measuring the performance of the formal services sector. With a 2024–25 base year, the ISP covers 19 service sub-sectors and will provide monthly insights into service sector activity, strengthening economic monitoring, national accounts estimation, and evidence-based policymaking. In its first trial release for April 2026, 14 of the 19 sub-sectors recorded double-digit growth, with Accommodation & Food Services emerging as the fastest-growing segment, recording 37.2% annual growth, followed by Retail Trade (30.8%), Administrative & Support Services (28.7%), Real Estate (27.7%), and Telecommunications (22.8%). As the services sector contributes over 50% of India's Gross Value Added (GVA) and nearly 30% of total employment, the ISP fills a crucial gap by providing a timely and reliable measure of India's largest economic sector.
